reforms.description: The Polish government is enhancing the skills of workers through a comprehensive skill strategy, which includes the establishment of the SME Manager Academy to finance training for managerial staff in SMEs and the Innovation Manager Academy aimed at increasing skills in innovation development. This strategy is essential for adapting to technological changes and supporting economic recovery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills is crucial for improving productivity and innovation capacity in the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The 2018 Act on Higher Education and Science has been implemented, and the academies are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in the approach to workforce skills and innovation management.
reforms.source_quote: A new type of work permit – the seasonal work permit entitling foreigners to work for 9 months over 12-month period – was introduced for employment in agriculture and seasonal branches.

reforms.description: The Research Network: Lukasiewicz was launched in 2019 to conduct scientific research, including industrial research, enhancing collaboration between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration between academia and industry is essential for translating research into practical applications and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Research Network was officially launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative significantly enhances existing collaboration frameworks but does not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Research Network: Lukasiewicz has been launched in 2019 to conduct scientific research, including industrial research.

reforms.description: Poland established a new national Broadband Fund in 2020 to accelerate the deployment of high-speed broadband in rural areas, with an annual budget of PLN 140 million.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital infrastructure is vital for enhancing connectivity and supporting innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational with allocated budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant investment in infrastructure but is not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: In addition, the authorities have set up a new national Broadband Fund in 2020 to accelerate the deployment of high-speed broadband in rural areas (PLN 140 million per year or 0.01% of 2018 GDP).

reforms.description: Poland plans to increase public health financing to a minimum of 6% of GDP by 2024, aiming to improve healthcare outcomes and capacity in response to the pandemic.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing health financing is essential for improving healthcare outcomes and supporting public health innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been officially announced and is set for implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing public health infrastructure and capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The planned increase in public health financing to a minimum of 6% of GDP by 2024 is welcome.

reforms.description: The Polish government plans to increase public spending on R&D by 0.3% of GDP, which is not financed by EU funds, as part of broader reforms to enhance R&D and innovation activities, including expanded tax incentives for SMEs and start-ups to support their R&D expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding and tax incentives is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan for increased R&D funding has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have undertaken reforms to ease administrative business and trade procedures and increase R&D and innovation activities.

reforms.description: Poland has implemented measures to ease business creation and growth, including lowering administrative requirements for new and smaller firms and introducing a simplified joint stock company with low capital requirements to facilitate start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Easing business creation and growth is essential for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been implemented and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent significant improvements in the startup ecosystem but do not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have recently implemented measures to ease business creation and growth, notably, lower administrative requirements for new and smaller firms.

reforms.description: The Polish Agency for Enterprise Development (PARP) has introduced a new development loan for micro and small companies to support their growth by financing the purchase of new fixed assets and software integration.
reforms.component_name: Development Loan for Micro and Small Companies
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing financial support for micro and small companies is crucial for fostering innovation and growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The development loan has been introduced and is available to eligible companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the loan represents a significant but not transformative change in support for enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: In 2019, to help small and young companies in gathering capital for their development, PARP introduced a new development loan for micro and small companies.

reforms.description: The Polish Investment and Trade Agency (PAIH) launched the Tech Bridges project to support the foreign expansion of start-ups and SMEs with high potential, funded by the European Regional Development Fund.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting the international expansion of start-ups is essential for enhancing their growth and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The Tech Bridges project has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The project represents a significant initiative to support start-ups but does not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The 2018 Tech Bridges project, funded by the European Regional Development Fund, supports foreign expansion of start-ups and SMEs with high potential.

reforms.description: Poland is launching 'Digital Technology Hubs' to promote digital innovation and knowledge transfer to SMEs, addressing previous challenges in cluster development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promoting digital innovation and knowledge transfer is essential for enhancing the competitiveness of SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced and is set for implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The launch of the hubs represents a significant initiative to support SMEs but does not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are launching 'Digital Technology Hubs' to promote digital innovation and knowledge transfer to SMEs.

reforms.description: Poland has launched a FinTech regulatory sandbox to facilitate the experimentation of new technologies in financial services, allowing firms to test products with reduced regulatory requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating experimentation in financial services is crucial for fostering innovation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulatory sandbox has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The sandbox represents a significant initiative to support innovation in FinTech but does not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Poland had launched in late 2018 a FinTech regulatory sandbox supervised by the Polish Financial Supervision Authority.

reforms.description: The Polish financial supervisor, KNF, is remodeling its regulatory sandbox approach to promote innovation in financial services, inspired by Sweden's model of constant dialogue with firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the regulatory framework for innovation in financial services is essential for fostering a conducive environment for new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The remodeling has been officially announced and is set for implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The remodeling represents a significant improvement in the regulatory approach but does not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The project has been suspended and is currently being remodeled.
